title: Minimalist Living Room
description: A light-filled living space shaped around soft seating, warm wood, and a calm neutral palette.
brief: The client wanted a relaxed room for hosting friends, reading, and everyday family time without losing the clean architectural feel of the house.
details: Layered natural textures to make the minimal palette feel warm.
Planned flexible seating for both quiet evenings and small gatherings.
Used low furniture to keep the window wall open and bright.

title: Stone & Oak Kitchen
description: A contemporary kitchen with practical storage, stone surfaces, and warm oak details.
brief: The goal was to make daily cooking easier while keeping the kitchen open, clean, and connected to the dining area.
details: Moved tall storage to one wall to free the main preparation zone.
Balanced durable stone with warmer oak to avoid a cold showroom feel.
Added layered lighting for preparation, dining, and evening mood.

title: Quiet Bedroom Suite
description: A peaceful bedroom suite designed with soft contrast, generous textiles, and hidden storage.
brief: The bedroom needed to feel restful and uncluttered while still supporting dressing, reading, and compact apartment storage.
details: Used muted wall color to frame the bed and reduce visual noise.
Built storage around daily routines rather than generic wardrobes.
Selected soft lighting so the room works from morning to late evening.

title: Spa Inspired Bathroom
description: A compact bathroom turned into a calm spa-like retreat with stone, glass, and warm lighting.
brief: The client wanted hotel-level comfort in a small footprint, with better storage and a softer morning routine.
details: Used large format surfaces to make the room feel wider.
Integrated niches and mirrored storage to remove visual clutter.
Specified warm dimmable lighting for a softer evening atmosphere.

title: Soft Dining Room
description: A generous dining area with tactile finishes, comfortable chairs, and a slower weekend mood.
brief: The space needed to work for weekday meals, long dinners, and occasional laptop work without feeling formal.
details: Chose rounded furniture to soften the room and improve circulation.
Layered a large pendant with wall lighting for flexible atmosphere.
Used washable textures that still feel refined and natural.

title: Loft Interior
description: An industrial loft softened with tailored furniture, art, warm textiles, and layered lighting.
brief: The client loved the raw shell but needed the open-plan space to feel comfortable, zoned, and more personal.
details: Created clear zones without closing down the open loft volume.
Balanced metal and concrete with linen, leather, and warm timber.
Curated oversized art to give the space identity and scale.

title: Japandi Bathroom Retreat
description: A quiet bathroom with timber accents, matte stone, and a very simple storage rhythm.
brief: The room needed to feel slower and more ritual-focused without becoming visually empty.
details: Paired warm timber with matte mineral surfaces.
Reduced visible hardware for a calmer first impression.
Added soft indirect lighting near the bathing zone.

title: Classic City Kitchen
description: A compact city kitchen with tailored cabinetry, gray stone, and polished brass details.
brief: The client needed a hardworking kitchen that still matched the apartment's older architectural language.
details: Used full-height cabinetry to increase storage.
Kept appliances integrated to preserve a classic rhythm.
Selected gray stone for durability and quiet contrast.

title: Terracotta Bedroom
description: A bedroom warmed with plaster tones, linen bedding, and hand-finished side tables.
brief: The space needed warmth and personality while staying restful enough for daily use.
details: Used terracotta as a grounding color rather than an accent.
Mixed linen, ceramic, and aged timber for tactility.
Kept the furniture low to make the ceiling feel taller.

title: Monochrome Powder Room
description: A small powder room with crisp black fixtures, white surfaces, and graphic lighting.
brief: The brief was to make a tiny room memorable while keeping maintenance simple.
details: Used contrast to create impact in a small footprint.
Specified easy-clean surfaces with minimal grout lines.
Added one sculptural fixture as the focal point.

title: Coastal Light Kitchen
description: An airy kitchen with pale timber, soft whites, and open shelving for relaxed hosting.
brief: The owners wanted the kitchen to feel bright and informal, with enough storage for seasonal family stays.
details: Used pale timber to keep the space relaxed.
Created one open display wall for everyday ceramics.
Balanced open shelving with concealed storage.

title: Green Reading Room
description: A compact sitting room with book storage, green walls, and layered task lighting.
brief: The room needed to become a quiet reading zone without disconnecting from the rest of the home.
details: Built shelving around the existing window.
Used a muted green to make the room feel calm and contained.
Layered table, floor, and picture lighting.

title: Scandinavian Family Kitchen
description: A family kitchen with simple cabinetry, durable surfaces, and a generous breakfast zone.
brief: The kitchen had to support cooking, homework, breakfast, and guests without becoming visually busy.
details: Separated prep and breakfast zones for easier daily use.
Used durable matte finishes for family traffic.
Kept visual lines simple and bright.

title: Industrial Guest Bedroom
description: A guest bedroom softened with charcoal textiles, warm leather, and simple metal details.
brief: The room needed to feel connected to the loft shell but still comfortable for longer stays.
details: Softened industrial finishes with layered textiles.
Added compact storage for weekend guests.
Kept the palette restrained and easy to maintain.

title: Earth Tone Primary Suite
description: A primary bedroom with grounded color, wool textures, and a quiet dressing corner.
brief: The owners wanted a restorative room that felt personal but not decorative.
details: Used earth tones to connect the room to the garden view.
Created a compact dressing corner with soft task lighting.
Selected wool and linen for subtle texture.

title: Cool Gray Bathroom
description: A clean bathroom with gray stone, recessed storage, and a precise lighting plan.
brief: The space needed to feel larger, sharper, and easier to use before busy workdays.
details: Used continuous gray surfaces to visually expand the room.
Recessed storage to reduce clutter around the sink.
Added bright task lighting and softer night lighting.

title: Japandi Living Gallery
description: A calm living room with sculptural storage, oak flooring, and a curated art wall.
brief: The client wanted a living area that worked as both a quiet lounge and a small art display.
details: Used low storage to keep the room visually open.
Balanced oak warmth with pale stone and linen.
Designed the wall layout around changing artwork.

title: Terracotta Kitchen Nook
description: A compact kitchen nook with clay-toned tile, open shelves, and soft morning light.
brief: The owners wanted a small but expressive kitchen corner for coffee, breakfast, and casual cooking.
details: Used clay tile as the main character of the space.
Added open shelves for everyday pieces and warm texture.
Kept the cabinetry simple so the palette could lead.
